tourism_hits.why: New Vision Zero Projects in Longmont: The City of Longmont is launching new initiatives aimed at improving transportation safety through its upcoming projects, activities and ways to get involved. Suggested next step: Investigate specific projects and activities, check for public participation opportunities and deadlines. What would make this publishable: Details on the specific projects, activities and ways to get involved.

tourism_hits.why: Technical Issues Affecting City of Longmont Online Permitting Portal: The online permitting portal for building services in longmontcolorado.gov is currently experiencing technical issues. Editor context: This could impact residents who are planning construction projects or renovations and need to use the city's official resources. Timely resolution can prevent disruptions during a busy period like summer when most people start their work on homes. Suggested treatment: watch. Newsworthiness: 13/20 (immediacy 5, impact 4, conflict 1, novelty 3). Why now: A technical issue has affected building services' online permitting portal in Longmont. The impact can be significant during busy periods like summer when residents start their work on homes or renovations. Suggested next step: Identify potential ways to mitigate disruptions, contact the city's IT department for an estimated time of repair and alternative resources. What would make this publishable: Information about the issue's origin, estimated time for fix and an alternative resource.

tourism_hits.why: Library Closed for Independence Day (July 4, 2026): The Longmont Public Library will be closed on July 3 and again from June 27 to August 2 in observance of the US Independence Day holiday. Editor context: Citizens need this information for planning activities around these closures, especially since they overlap with summer programs like Kids Club. Newsworthiness: 12/20 (immediacy 5, impact 4, conflict 1, novelty 2). Why now: No current change found as it is a scheduled event based on historical data. Suggested next step: Notify library patrons of the closure dates and any changes to regular services during this period.

linked_evidence.excerpt:
Discover all the departments that make up the City of Longmont’s government. Get detailed information on their roles, responsibilities, and how they serve the community.
All Departments
Government
Home
City Clerk
Agenda Management Portal
Agenda Management Portal
More in City Clerk
Agenda Management Portal    Business License    Colorado Revised Statutes    Election Information    Campaign Finance Reporting Dashboard (Beta)    Candidate Information - How to run for City Council    Fair Campaign Practices and Required Reporting    Initiative, Referendum and Recall    Past Election Results    Voter Registration Information      Licenses and Permits    Animal Licenses    Fishing Permits    Liquor License Application Forms    Liquor Licenses    Marijuana Licenses    Special Events Permit    Specialty Business Licenses      Longmont Municipal Code    Transparency, Public Records and Open Records Requests
Welcome to the new Public Meeting Portal
All agendas and meeting packets of the Longmont City Council and the City’s advisory boards are published to this portal to make it easier for residents to find information about upcoming meetings and topics.
Not all meetings listed here are livestreamed. Generally, City Council and Planning & Zoning Commission meetings are livestreamed, but not all of them are.  Look for the words “(livestreamed)” after the meeting title.
Most advisory board meetings are recorded and uploaded to the City’s YouTube channel a few days after the meeting (youtube.com/@cityoflongmont).
Search for past topics by using the Search and Advanced Search functions.
The portal page works best in Google Chrome.  Some users may not see the page fully load if using Internet Explorer.   Click to view this page in a separate window .

Why now: No current change found. Suggested next step: Confirm if there are any age restrictions or special requirements to attend these programs. city_nav_hits.why: Council vote on Tuesday for roof contract at Longmont Library: The City Council's agenda includes a significant decision to proceed with contracting work necessary before winter, as well as an announcement of two new library programs. Editor context: Council action on city contract can affect public funds and services; the addition of potential community involvement through library program announcements makes this timely. Suggested treatment: story. Why now: The agenda includes a specific vote on the library roof work project scheduled for Tuesday with public participation invited at 10am. Suggested next step: Verify council members' positions, confirm meeting date details, check contract scope and budget. Then attach or cite the source document before drafting.
city_nav_hits.confirmation_checklist: ["Verify council members' positions, confirm meeting date details, check contract scope and budget. Then attach or cite the source document before drafting.","Confirm dates, names, dollar amounts, and decision points before publication.","Keep unverified or single-source claims labeled for editor review."]
city_nav_hits.novelty_reason: The agenda includes a specific vote on the library roof work project scheduled for Tuesday with public participation invited at 10am.

city_nav_hits.why: Longmont Public Library Programs Announcement: New programming initiatives have been announced by Longmont's City Council, which include an expanded summer camp program and tech classes aimed to attract families. Editor context: These new programs represent a significant opportunity for community involvement as well as potential economic impact through increased library attendance. Suggested treatment: watch. Why now: New programs announced by City Council aimed at increasing family engagement with Longmont Library services. Suggested next step: Review details of the summer camp and tech classes, explore initial interest or sign-ups if any.
city_nav_hits.confirmation_checklist: ["Review details of the summer camp and tech classes, explore initial interest or sign-ups if any.","Confirm dates, names, dollar amounts, and decision points before publication.","Keep unverified or single-source claims labeled for editor review."]

city_nav_hits.why: Technical Issues Affecting City of Longmont Online Permitting Portal: The online permitting portal for building services in longmontcolorado.gov is currently experiencing technical issues. Editor context: This could impact residents who are planning construction projects or renovations and need to use the city's official resources. Timely resolution can prevent disruptions during a busy period like summer when most people start their work on homes. Suggested treatment: watch. Newsworthiness: 13/20 (immediacy 5, impact 4, conflict 1, novelty 3). Why now: A technical issue has affected building services' online permitting portal in Longmont. The impact can be significant during busy periods like summer when residents start their work on homes or renovations. Suggested next step: Identify potential ways to mitigate disruptions, contact the city's IT department for an estimated time of repair and alternative resources. What would make this publishable: Information about the issue's origin, estimated time for fix and an alternative resource.
city_nav_hits.confirmation_checklist: ["Identify potential ways to mitigate disruptions, contact the city's IT department for an estimated time of repair and alternative resources. What would make this publishable: Information about the issue's origin, estimated time for fix and an alternative resource.","Confirm dates, names, dollar amounts, and decision points before publication.","Keep unverified or single-source claims labeled for editor review."]
city_nav_hits.novelty_reason: A technical issue has affected building services' online permitting portal in Longmont. The impact can be significant during busy periods like summer when residents start their work on homes or renovations.

city_nav_hits.why: Free Summer Reading Challenge at Longmont Public Library: The City of Longmont is hosting its annual summer reading challenge from May 21 through July 31, where participants need to hand in their boards and cards for prizes. Editor context: It's an educational event with a deadline that affects community members. Suggested treatment: brief. Newsworthiness: 10/20 (immediacy 2, impact 3, conflict 1, novelty 4). Why now: No current change found as it is an annual recurring event. Suggested next step: Confirm the closing date for board and card submissions, reach out to participants or organizers if necessary.
